title: pyrus aucuparia f. praemorsa
intro: Pyrus aucuparia f. praemorsa, also known as the Rowan or Mountain Ash, is a deciduous shrub or small tree with white flowers and red berries. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can aid in treating wounds and infections. The Rowan tree is said to have various health benefits, including its ability to support immune function and provide antioxidant protection, potentially helping to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and cancer. Herbal preparations of the Rowan tree, such as teas and tinctures, can be made from its berries, bark, and leaves, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
